regions.us-ports: Today Miami reports N 2kt winds, partly cloudy skies, and a 20% rain probability, supporting on-time embarkation and routine shore-excursion boarding with minimal wind constraints. Over the next 24 hours Miami is forecast NNE 8kt with thunderstorms and a 20% rain probability, and short-lived thunderstorms at NNE 8kt may necessitate brief sheltering or temporary gangway pauses at Miami, Port Everglades, and Port Canaveral. By the second day the forecast shifts to SW 11kt with drizzle and a 17% rain probability, so vessels and shoreside teams should plan for SW 11kt winds during maneuvering and tender operations in Tampa Bay and Port Canaveral. Beyond 48 hours the outlook shows SE 11kt with thunderstorms and a 26% rain probability, and the SE 11kt wind vector should be considered when setting departure routing and tender schedules from Miami and Port Everglades.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today SE 4kt, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability are reported for Nassau, so embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions at Nassau and Freeport are proceeding under light SE 4kt winds and clear skies. Over the next 24 hours Day 1 shows SE 8kt and overcast with less than 10% rain probability for Nassau, with those Day 1 conditions expected during scheduled port operations at Bimini, the Berry Islands, and Key West. In the 24–48 hour window Day 2 is forecast ESE 12kt and overcast with less than 10% rain probability for Nassau, and ESE 12kt winds on Day 2 should be factored into maneuvering, tendering, and dock operations at Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 indicates SE 13kt, drizzle, and 13% rain probability for Nassau, and the stronger SE 13kt wind and drizzle with 13% rain probability coincide with planned calls at Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today SE 5kt with partly cloudy skies and 13% rain probability, and the light SE 5kt supports routine embarkation and disembarkation operations at San Juan and St. Thomas with minimal wind-related restrictions on tendering. Over the next 24–48 hours Day 1 is forecast E 14kt with thunderstorms and 13% rain probability and Day 2 is forecast E 15kt with thunderstorms and 18% rain probability, and those E 14kt–E 15kt winds should be accounted for in docking and shore-excursion timing at San Juan, St. Croix, and St. Thomas. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 trends toward E 17kt with drizzle and 11% rain probability, and the increase to E 17kt could raise vessel motion during approaches and affect open-deck embarkation at St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Crews and excursion planners should track the sky condition shift from the current partly cloudy to thunderstorms on Day 1 and Day 2 and drizzle on Day 3 alongside the wind rise from SE 5kt to E 17kt when finalizing operational plans for the listed ports.
regions.western-caribbean: Today Cozumel reports NE 2 kt, overcast, and less than 10% rain probability; light NE winds with overcast skies support normal embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursion staging in Cozumel. Over the next 24 hours Cozumel shifts to ENE 8 kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, and Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City should include the ENE 8 kt wind component and thunderstorm wording in tendering and pier operation plans. In the 24–48 hour window Cozumel is forecast ENE 8 kt with thunderstorms and 13% rain probability on day 2, maintaining an ENE 8 kt wind component relevant to gangway safety and outdoor excursion staging at Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City. Beyond 48 hours Cozumel day 3 outlook is ENE 7 kt with thunderstorms and 13% rain probability, indicating persistent light ENE winds and recurring thunderstorm wording that operations and shore excursion scheduling should incorporate for Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today Priority Alert: elevated E 22kt winds today and elevated E 21kt winds on day 2 are forecast to most affect Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena. Today’s current conditions show ESE 18kt, partly cloudy, and less than 10% rain probability; with the Day 1 forecast of E 22kt and drizzle, embarkation, tendering, and exposed shore excursions at Aruba and Curacao should anticipate increased vessel motion and require extra safety attention due to the stronger E and ESE winds. Over the next 24-48 hours, Day 2 guidance shows E 21kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability for Aruba, and similar E 21kt wind potential for Bonaire and Cartagena that could restrict tendering or gangway use and prompt use of alternate embarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, Day 3 trends show easing to E 18kt with drizzle and 11% rain probability for Aruba, indicating lower sustained E winds than earlier in the outlook while still requiring wind-aware operations at Aruba, Bonaire, and Curacao.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today a Priority Alert: Day 2 thunderstorms with east winds 15 kt and a 60% rain probability are expected to affect port operations and shore excursions in Barbados, St. Lucia and Martinique. Today Barbados reports east winds 10 kt, clear skies and a 28% rain probability, and the east 10 kt and clear skies support scheduled embarkation and tendering at Barbados and nearby calls such as Antigua. Over the next 24-48 hours an east wind of 15 kt with thunderstorms and a 60% rain probability is expected to affect tendering and outdoor excursions in Barbados, St. Lucia and Martinique, so vessels should plan for thunderstorm-related delays given the forecast east 15 kt. Beyond 48 hours Barbados trends toward east winds 14 kt with drizzle and a 13% rain probability, and the east 14 kt forecast should be factored into maneuvering and tendering plans for calls including Grenada and St. Vincent.
regions.los-angeles: Today winds are W 4kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing routine embarkation and disembarkation at Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, and Marina del Rey with minimal wind-related delays to tendering and gangway operations. Over the next 24 hours (Day 1) conditions shift to W 11kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, which could produce slightly choppier water for Catalina Island tenders and open-boat shore excursions from Long Beach. Between 24 and 48 hours (Day 2) the forecast increases to WSW 12kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, so Los Angeles / San Pedro and Marina del Rey should plan for steady WSW 12kt winds affecting deck and gangway handling. Beyond 48 hours (Day 3) winds ease to SW 9kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, indicating lighter SW 9kt winds and clearer skies that will generally ease tendering for Catalina Island and evening shore excursion schedules.
regions.ensenada: Today SE 1kt, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability support routine embarkation and shore excursions at Ensenada and Punta Banda with minimal wind-related restrictions on gangways and open decks. Beyond 48 hours (Day 3) WSW 9kt, overcast, and less than 10% rain probability point to sustained light west-southwest winds and overcast skies for Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos, supporting continued scheduled shore excursions absent operational constraints.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today NW 7kt winds, partly cloudy skies, and less than 10% rain probability support routine embarkation and shore excursion operations at Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo, with gangway and tender transfers operating without wind-related restrictions. Over the next 24 hours a W 10kt forecast with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability will be in place for scheduled calls at Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo and should allow normal pier operations. regions.mazatlan: Today NNW 5kt, overcast, 16% rain probability, with light NNW winds at 5 kt and persistent overcast skies allowing routine embarkation, disembarkation, and scheduled shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. regions.puerto-vallarta: Today NNE 2kt, partly cloudy, 17% rain probability; light NNE 2kt winds and partly cloudy skies support routine embarkation and disembarkation operations at Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita with a 17% rain probability noted. regions.western-mediterranean: Today WNW 3kt, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability; light WNW 3kt winds and clear skies support routine embarkation and disembarkation at Barcelona with minimal wind-related constraints on gangways. In the next 24-48 hours Day 1 SSW 10kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 SSW 11kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability will be relevant for Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza when scheduling tendering and shore excursion boarding. regions.central-mediterranean: Today Naples reports W 2kt, overcast, 23% rain probability; light west winds and overcast skies support routine embarkation and disembarkation at Naples and Civitavecchia with no wind-related tendering restrictions expected. Over the next 24 hours Day 1 forecasts SE 6kt, rain showers, 23% rain probability in Naples; vessels and shore excursion teams scheduled for Naples, Livorno, and Civitavecchia should note the SE 6kt wind and rain showers in the forecast. Between 24 and 48 hours Day 2 shows NE 9kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability for Naples; NE 9kt and persistent overcast conditions will be the dominant forecast for Genoa and La Spezia approaches. regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today SW 4kt, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability; harbor conditions at Athens (Piraeus) support routine embarkation and disembarkation and shore excursions at Piraeus and Santorini under light SW winds. regions.southeast-alaska: Today Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway are facing rain showers; Juneau current conditions are ESE 2kt and partly cloudy with an 85% rain probability and the Day 1 forecast of SE 4kt with rain showers and an 85% rain probability is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Juneau is forecast ESE 8kt with drizzle and a 64% rain probability that is expected to impact shore-excursion timing and embarkation procedures for Juneau, and operators in Ketchikan and Skagway should account for wetter conditions. Beyond 48 hours Juneau is forecast SSW 4kt with drizzle and a 42% rain probability, which may affect tendering and outdoor visits in Juneau and could influence scheduling at Sitka or Haines. Vessel operations and gangway planning should note Juneau’s low wind regime from ESE 2kt current to SE 4kt Day 1 along with the 85% rain probability today and the 64% rain probability on Day 2 when assigning gangway teams and wet-weather gear for passengers and crew.
